name: Conrad Gozzo
abstract: Conrad Joseph Gozzo (February 6, 1922 – October 8, 1964) was an American trumpet player. He was a member of the NBC Hollywood staff orchestra at the time of his death.

works.t: Strike Up the Band: The Man I Love

about_quotes.speaker: Rusty Dedrick
about_quotes.evidence: And you know my trumpet mate was Conrad Gozzo. And we were kids together, teenagers, and sitting and playing with him all the time... I roomed with Conrad for two and a half years. We left Red Norvo’s band to go with Claude Thornhill’s band. We left together.
about_quotes.doc_title: Rusty Dedrick interviewed by Monk Rowe, Ellenville, New York, June 23, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Pete Candoli
about_quotes.evidence: In fact Goz was my closest friend. I’m his first child’s Godfather.
about_quotes.doc_title: Pete Candoli interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Artie Shaw
about_quotes.evidence: I went around and saw a lot of trumpet players and trombone players and saxophone players and got them together. There was . . . Claude Thornhill came down. Sam Donahue. Conrad Gozzo. Maxie Kaminsky. It was a hell of a band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Artie Shaw — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Hardaway
about_quotes.evidence: Conrad Gozzo was still there and he was famous, one of the most famous lead trumpet players, number one in town for many years.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Hardaway interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: And then in the trumpet section, they had Conrad Gozzo and Harry James and I think Charlie Shavers was in that trumpet section.

about_quotes.speaker: Lew Soloff
about_quotes.evidence: I got my conception of lead trumpet playing from three guys basically: From Conrad Gozzo and from Ernie Royal
about_quotes.doc_title: Lew Soloff interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, April 16, 2000
